stem: In a fitness magazine, a 150-pound adult is estimated to use 180 calories for every 30 minutes of jogging and 240 calories for every 30 minutes of swimming. A 150-pound adult has completed 1 hour of jogging. Based on the magazine, how many hours should the adult swim to use a total of 1,800 calories from jogging and swimming?
rationale: One hour of jogging uses 2 × 180 = 360 calories. The remaining 1,800 - 360 = 1,440 calories must come from swimming. Each hour of swimming uses 2 × 240 = 480 calories, so the swimming time is 1,440 / 480 = 3 hours. Choice B is correct.
